title: Neuburgia moluccana
taxonomy: Neuburgia moluccana is classified within the family Menispermaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as moonseed or menispermaceous plants. This family consists of around 150 species, distributed across 15 genera. Neuburgia moluccana is specifically classified within the genus Neuburgia, which contains approximately 14 species of flowering plants native to the Indo-Pacific region. The species is further classified as Neuburgia moluccana, with the specific epithet referring to its native distribution in the Moluccas. Neuburgia moluccana is a member of the clade Magnoliophyta, which encompasses all flowering plants.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a promising natural remedy for various ailments.
